source.multipart_basis: Decomposition reflects the 200 x 150 x 150 mm 3D-printed PETG stratospheric payload enclosure with M6 parachute eyelet, internal electronics, and solar-exposed top face described in the original brief. Separated bodies cover aeroshell top (with eyelet reinforcement), aeroshell bottom (impact face), internal payload deck, parachute canister / eyelet insert, recovery bay, battery bracket, and antenna mount. No rulebook citations added beyond those carried by the original sample.

full_prompt: Deliver the primary enclosure of a Technion Aerospace Faculty student-challenge sounding-balloon stratospheric payload as a multi-body assembly rather than a single solid. The payload rides under a latex sounding balloon with ~30 km AGL burst altitude and descends under a 0.9 m parachute at 6 m/s sea-level terminal rate. Envelope 200 x 150 x 150 mm; wet mass 1.2 kg (batteries, camera, radio, sensors). Housing is 3D-printed PETG (E 2.1 GPa; tensile 45 MPa at 20 C, 40 MPa at -40 C; Tg = 85 C; rho 1270 kg/m^3). Walls are 3 mm with internal reinforcing ribs. Stratospheric environment: ambient 1 kPa absolute at 30 km, -60 C, 1360 W/m^2 solar on sun-exposed face, 5 W internal dissipation. Parachute attachment is an M6 eyelet at the top face. Load cases (unchanged): (LC1) 5 g handling / drop on 1.2 kg at the eyelet (60 N tensile equivalent); (LC2) 150 N parachute-opening shock at the eyelet axial; (LC3) 3 g burst shock on 1.2 kg at CoM; (LC4) 10 g ground impact deceleration on 1.2 kg onto the bottom face; (LC5) steady stratospheric thermal at 30 km with -60 C ambient, 1360 W/m^2 solar on top face, 5 W internal, convection negligible. Pass/fail (unchanged): (R1) under LC1-LC4 at -40 C cold-soak max stress <= 20 MPa (40 / 2.0 FoS); (R2) under LC5 internal-air temperature >= 0 C AND sun-exposed surface temperature <= 65 C (Tg - 20 C margin); (R3) eyelet pull-out local stress under LC2 <= 20 MPa in PETG near the insert; (R4) enclosure dry mass (walls + ribs only) <= 250 g; (R5) differential pressure 100 kPa outward (101 kPa internal vs 1 kPa external) produces wall deflection < 2 mm. In this multi-part rendering the enclosure is decomposed into: (A) aeroshell top (sun-exposed face with eyelet boss reinforcement; 1 body), (B) aeroshell bottom (impact face reacting LC4 ground impact; 1 body), (C) internal payload deck (horizontal floor that stacks electronics between top and bottom shells; 1 body), (D) parachute canister / eyelet insert (metal insert heat-staked or epoxy-bonded into the top shell boss; 1 body), (E) recovery bay (short vertical sub-compartment above the deck containing parachute + release hardware; 1 body), (F) battery bracket (internal bracket on the deck holding battery pack; 1 body), (G) antenna mount (external mount on a side wall for antenna whip; 1 body). Inter-part connections: aeroshell top and bottom bolted through the internal deck at the four corners, eyelet insert epoxied and heat-staked into top boss, recovery bay bonded to deck and keyed into top shell, battery bracket bolted to deck, antenna mount screwed to side wall of top or bottom shell. Final FEM verification runs on the ASSEMBLED enclosure; per-part stress is reported but does not gate pass/fail. Inter-part connection DCR must be <= 1.0 under governing load case (LC2 parachute shock for top path; LC4 ground impact for bottom path). All inherited load magnitudes, material properties, and pass/fail thresholds are preserved verbatim; assembly-scope criterion R_asm1 is added. Deliverables: STEP AP242 multi-body model, rib schedule, mechanical FEA at 20 C and -40 C, steady-state thermal with solar + radiation BCs, pressure-differential FEA, mass budget, per-connection DCR table.
short_prompt:
Deliver the primary enclosure of a Technion Aerospace Faculty student-challenge sounding-balloon stratospheric payload as a multi-body assembly. Payload rides under a latex sounding balloon with ~30 km AGL burst altitude and descends under a 0.9 m parachute at 6 m/s sea-level terminal rate.

Envelope: 200 x 150 x 150 mm; wet mass 1.2 kg (batteries, camera, radio, sensors). Parachute attachment is an M6 eyelet at the top face. Walls 3 mm with internal reinforcing ribs.

Material: 3D-printed PETG (tensile 45 MPa at 20 C, 40 MPa at -40 C; Tg = 85 C). Stratospheric environment: ambient 1 kPa absolute at 30 km, -60 C, 1360 W/m^2 solar on sun-exposed face, 5 W internal dissipation.

Load cases: (LC1) 5 g handling/drop on 1.2 kg at eyelet (60 N tensile equivalent); (LC2) 150 N parachute-opening shock axial at eyelet; (LC3) 3 g burst shock on 1.2 kg at CoM; (LC4) 10 g ground impact on 1.2 kg onto bottom face; (LC5) steady stratospheric thermal at 30 km, -60 C ambient, 1360 W/m^2 solar on top face, 5 W internal, convection negligible.

Pass/fail: (R1) LC1-LC4 at -40 C cold-soak max stress <= 20 MPa (40/2.0 FoS); (R2) LC5 internal-air >= 0 C AND sun-exposed surface <= 65 C; (R3) eyelet pull-out stress under LC2 <= 20 MPa in PETG near insert; (R4) enclosure dry mass (walls + ribs) <= 250 g; (R5) 100 kPa differential pressure outward produces wall deflection < 2 mm.

prompt.title: Technion Stratospheric Balloon Payload Enclosure (multi-part assembly)
prompt.brief: 3D-printed PETG stratospheric balloon payload enclosure 200 x 150 x 150 mm, 1.2 kg wet, -60 C / 1 kPa / 1360 W/m^2 environment. Multi-part: aeroshell top + bottom, internal payload deck, M6 eyelet insert / parachute canister, recovery bay, battery bracket, antenna mount. FEM on assembled enclosure, connection DCR <= 1.0.

load_cases.name: handling_5g
load_cases.application: 5 g on 1.2 kg at parachute eyelet, any direction

load_cases.name: burst_shock_3g
load_cases.application: impulse at CoM from balloon release

load_cases.name: ground_impact_10g
load_cases.application: decel on bottom face at 6 m/s impact

load_cases.name: stratospheric_thermal
load_cases.application: steady -60 C ambient, 1360 W/m^2 solar on top, 5 W internal, negligible convection

parts.name: aeroshell_top
parts.role: upper shell with sun-exposed face, eyelet boss, and solar-thermal gate R2 governing; hosts antenna mount boss

parts.name: aeroshell_bottom
parts.role: lower shell with impact face reacting LC4 ground impact; hosts battery bracket bolt-downs

parts.name: payload_deck
parts.role: internal horizontal floor stacking electronics; ties top and bottom shells at four corner bosses

parts.name: parachute_eyelet_insert
parts.role: metal M6 eyelet insert heat-staked or epoxy-bonded into top boss; carries LC2 150 N parachute shock

parts.name: recovery_bay
parts.role: short vertical sub-compartment above the payload deck housing parachute pack and release hardware

parts.name: battery_bracket
parts.role: internal bracket on payload deck holding the battery pack; bolted to deck

parts.name: antenna_mount
parts.role: external mount on side wall for antenna whip or patch; screwed into a side-wall boss

assembly_envelope.coordinate_system: x along 200 mm side, y along 150 mm side, z vertical (eyelet +); origin at bottom-face centre

pass_fail_criteria.derivation: 100 kPa outward differential (101 kPa internal vs 1 kPa external)

pass_fail_criteria.description: Connection demand-to-capacity ratio (DCR) across all inter-part interfaces (IF1..IF6) <= 1.0 under governing load case (LC2 parachute shock and LC4 ground impact).

pass_fail_criteria.derivation: Generated coverage gate: ensures prompt-level envelope, body-count, report-field, and part geometry constraints are represented by an explicit geometry audit experiment.

recommended_fea_setup.element_type: shell for A/B/C/E/G; solid TET10 near eyelet insert D; bolt preload on corner bosses between A/C/B
analysis_types: linear static at 20 C and -40 C (LC1-LC4)

interfaces.load_path: top shell bolted to payload deck at 4 corner bosses; carries parachute shock LC2 into deck
bottom shell bolted to payload deck at 4 corner bosses; carries LC4 impact back into deck
M6 eyelet insert bonded into top-face boss; directly carries LC2 parachute shock
recovery bay bonded to deck and keyed into top shell interior
battery bracket bolted to deck; reacts battery inertial load under LC3/LC4
antenna mount screwed to top shell side wall; non-primary load path

fem_check_policy.rationale: Pressure-differential wall deflection (R5), eyelet pull-out (R3), and interior thermal distribution (R2) all depend on the assembled shell-plus-deck stiffness and on the insert-shell bond. Per-part stress is reported for visibility but does not gate pass/fail.

notes.why_multi_part: Original brief describes a 3D-printed PETG box with internal ribs, an M6 metal eyelet insert, internal electronics stack, and antenna mounting; modelling as a single solid collapses the bolted corner bosses, the bonded eyelet insert, and the internal deck that carries R3 and R5.
notes.speculation_caveats: Recovery-bay and antenna-mount presence are speculative; eyelet insert material (stainless vs Al) and bracket materials are team-selected.

generated_eval_coverage.purpose: Add runnable CCX coverage kit for JSON-only multipart prompt.
generated_eval_coverage.note: Generated checks assert solver coverage for every FEA-grade requirement, every declared load case, and prompt-level geometry constraints; they are not a domain-specific certification model.
